author: Retribution98
title: Smart CI fails: fastcore 2.0.1 breaks ghapi 1.0.4 - L.starmap AttributeError

failed_jobs.name: Smart_CI
failed_jobs.symptom: AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'starmap' when initializing GhApi client in smart_ci.py line 190

failed_jobs.name: ci/gha_overall_status_linux_riscv
failed_jobs.symptom: Overall status check exits 1 because Smart_CI failed

key_errors:
File "/home/runner/.local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/ghapi/core.py", line 105, in __init__
    funcs_ = L(funcs).starmap(_GhVerb, client=self, kwargs=kwargs)
             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/home/runner/.local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/fastcore/basics.py", line 578, in __getattr__
    if attr is not None: return getattr(attr,k)
                                ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'starmap'
root_cause: fastcore 2.0.1 (released ~2026-07-08) changed the behavior of the L class such that L(funcs) no longer returns a fastcore.L instance with the starmap() method; instead it returns a plain Python list. The ghapi==1.0.4 library's GhApi.__init__ (core.py line 105) calls L(funcs).starmap(_GhVerb, ...) and therefore raises AttributeError. The Smart CI step uses ghapi to query PR labels/components and fails immediately on initialization. The requirements.txt pins ghapi==1.0.4 but only specifies fastcore>=1.5.4, which allowed pip to resolve fastcore-2.0.1. This is the 6th occurrence of the same failure today (since 07:11 UTC), affecting multiple PRs in the merge queue.
reproduction_steps: pip install 'fastcore>=2.0.1' 'ghapi==1.0.4' && python3 -c "from ghapi.all import GhApi; GhApi()"
recommended_actions: Pin fastcore to <2.0.1 in .github/actions/smart-ci/requirements.txt (e.g., change 'fastcore>=1.5.4' to 'fastcore>=1.5.4,<2.0.0')
OR upgrade ghapi to a version compatible with fastcore 2.0.1
This fix is URGENT: the same error has blocked 6 merge-queue runs today (since 07:11 UTC) and affects all PRs in the RISC-V and other Linux pipelines that use Smart CI
prevention_strategies: Pin all transitive dependencies (including fastcore) to exact or capped versions in CI requirements files. Add a nightly check that verifies Smart CI can initialize successfully without running a full merge-queue pipeline.
historical_context: This is occurrence #6 of this exact failure today (2026-07-09). First seen at 07:11 UTC. Prior occurrences affected PRs #36780, #36773, #36504, #36781. Pattern hash: 7862e85dd17a5df8.
